description: Two algebraic equations form a non-trivial cycle in the assignment graph: A is defined in terms of B, and B is defined in terms of A, with no time derivative on either side. Bindings MUST detect the cycle and fail-fast with a structured error during discretize() / simulate() / mtkcompile() (Julia: structural_simplify reports algebraic-loop or singular-system; Python earthsci_toolkit: should raise rather than silently use defaults; Rust/TS/Go: per-binding error class). Silent success producing default values for A and B is a test failure.

metadata.name: CyclicAlgebraic
metadata.description: esm-sph fixture E: two-variable algebraic cycle that bindings must reject.

expect.rationale: A = B + 1, B = 2*A → A = 2*A + 1 → A = -1, B = -2. Although a fixed-point exists, the cycle has no acyclic topological assignment order, and discretize() / mtkcompile() must either solve the implicit system as a DAE/nonlinear block or fail-fast. A binding that silently assigns A = B + 1 with B at its default 0 and B = 2*A with A at its default 0 (yielding A=1, B=0 — INCONSISTENT with both equations) is the silent-failure mode this fixture is designed to catch.

note: All bindings are listed as optional in this initial landing — runner integration is filed as per-binding follow-up beads once the audit surfaces actual behavior. The fixture exists so the per-binding audits have a single shared input to point at; silent-success behavior found during the audit is the bug, not the fixture.
